checks.a_analysis: The output content accurately reflects the text found in the source document without inventing new sections, titles, or body text. The document title and image alt text are acceptable AI-generated additions for accessibility.
The only URL provided in the output, 'http://it.nc.gov/gicc', matches the source document exactly. No phone numbers, email addresses, or physical addresses were fabricated.

All main sections of the single-page agenda (Header, Agenda Table, Meeting Dates, Website Footer) are present in the output. While specific content details are missing within item 8, no whole section or page was omitted.

The source table contains a header row and 11 numbered agenda items, totaling 12 rows. The JSON output's table element (reading_order 6) also contains 12 rows. The row count is correct despite internal content omission issues.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: There is a significant omission of content regarding Agenda Item 8 ('Committee Reports'). The output fails to capture the names of the presenters associated with each committee (Ryan Draughn, Kathryn Clifton, John Farley, Doug Newcomb, Dan Madding, Stan Duncan) which are present in the 'GICC MEMBER or GUEST' column of the source. Additionally, two sub-agenda items indicating votes—'Vote: Standard for Parcel Data Content (Summary)' and 'Vote: LGC Bylaws Revised'—are entirely missing from the output. This represents a loss of more than 25% of the information for this multi-part agenda section.
checks.b_evidence_source:
• Statewide Mapping Advisory Committee Ryan Draughn
  o Vote: Standard for Parcel Data Content (Summary)
• Local Government Kathryn Clifton
  o Vote: LGC Bylaws Revised
• State Government GIS Users John Farley
• Federal Interagency Doug Newcomb
• Technical Advisory Dan Madding
• Management & Operations Stan Duncan
checks.b_evidence_output: The list element contains only committee names: "Statewide Mapping Advisory Committee", "Local Government", "State Government GIS Users", "Federal Interagency", "Technical Advisory", "Management & Operations".

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text that was successfully converted preserves the original meaning and wording of the source document, including dates, names (for other items), and specific terminology.

checks.a_analysis: The conversion results show no evidence of duplicated content. Each section of the agenda, including headers, paragraphs, and table entries, appears exactly once in the output, mirroring the source document.

The heading hierarchy is logical and normalized for accessibility. The document title elements 'North Carolina' and 'GEOGRAPHIC INFORMATION COORDINATING COUNCIL' are assigned H1 levels, and the section 'AGENDA' is assigned H2. This is an appropriate structure for a single-page document.

The table structure is corrupted at Row 8 ('Committee Reports'). In the source PDF, this row contains a complex nested list of committees and their respective speakers (GICC members/guests). In the output JSON, Row 8 is effectively empty in the 'GICC MEMBER or GUEST' column, and the content for the committees has been extracted into a separate list element later in the reading order. Furthermore, the personnel names (e.g., Ryan Draughn, Kathryn Clifton) associated with the committees are entirely missing from the table structure.
checks.b_evidence_source: Row 8: 8. Committee Reports • Statewide Mapping Advisory Committee Ryan Draughn ... • Management & Operations Stan Duncan
checks.b_evidence_output: The row at index 8 of the table (reading_order 6) has an empty data cell for 'GICC MEMBER or GUEST'.

checks.a_analysis: The reading order is significantly broken. The details belonging to Agenda Item 8 ('Committee Reports') are placed in a separate list (reading_order 7) that follows the entire table (reading_order 6). However, the table contains items 9, 10, and 11. This means a user reading top-to-bottom encounters item 8, skips to items 9, 10, and 11, and only then sees the specific committees listed under item 8. Additionally, the logo, which is the very first element in the source PDF, is placed at the end (reading_order 11).
checks.b_evidence_source: The source document shows the logo at the top, and committee details immediately following '8. Committee Reports' before moving to '9. Statewide Orthoimagery Program'.
checks.b_evidence_output: The list of committees (element index 7) follows the end of the table which includes items 9, 10, and 11. The logo (element index 11) is the last item.

checks.a_analysis: The nested list under '8. Committee Reports' is converted poorly. The original list is multi-level (containing committees and sub-items for 'Vote') and includes right-aligned names. The conversion has flattened the list, removed the secondary 'Vote' items entirely, and omitted all the personnel names (Ryan Draughn, Kathryn Clifton, etc.).
checks.b_evidence_source:
• Statewide Mapping Advisory Committee Ryan Draughn
o Vote: Standard for Parcel Data Content (Summary)
checks.b_evidence_output: The list at reading_order 7 only contains the top-level committee names: 'Statewide Mapping Advisory Committee', 'Local Government', etc.

checks.a_analysis: The source document contains several clickable links/URLs identified by color (blue) and underlining. The output fails to capture these as interactive links. Specifically, the link for 'Minutes', the two 'Vote' links, and the website URL at the bottom are all rendered as plain text without URI metadata.
checks.b_evidence_source: Minutes; Standard for Parcel Data Content (Summary); LGC Bylaws Revised; http://it.nc.gov/gicc
checks.b_evidence_output: No link objects or URI metadata provided in the paragraph or cell content for these items.

checks.a_analysis: The visual organization of Agenda Item 8 ('Committee Reports') is significantly altered. In the source PDF, this item uses a multi-column table layout to associate specific committees with their respective members (e.g., 'Statewide Mapping Advisory Committee' is horizontally aligned with 'Ryan Draughn'). In the HTML output, this organizational relationship is destroyed: the committee names are ejected from the table into a simple bulleted list below it, and the associated member names are missing entirely from the visual field. This collapses a meaningful multi-column relationship into a disjointed list, which confuses the meaning of the document.
checks.b_evidence_source: Agenda Item 8 'Committee Reports' shows a nested list with members in the third column (e.g., 'Statewide Mapping Advisory Committee' aligned with 'Ryan Draughn').
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML table row 8 is effectively empty for the member column, and the committees are listed in a plain bulleted list below the table without their associated members.

checks.a_analysis: The agenda table is visually broken because row 8 is not correctly rendered. While rows 1-7 and 9-11 appear correctly formatted, row 8 is missing its sub-content and the content in the 'GICC MEMBER or GUEST' column. The data that should be inside the table cells for row 8 has been partially rendered as a separate list below the table, meaning a sighted user cannot read or understand the relationships intended by the table structure for that specific section.
checks.b_evidence_source: A bordered table where row 8 contains a sub-list of committees and their corresponding members in separate columns.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML table shows row 8 with an empty third column, and the content that should be in the 'AGENDA ITEM' column is truncated/moved to a list below the table.

checks.a_analysis: The meaningful image from the source (the NCGICC logo) is present in the HTML output. Although its position has changed from the top of the page to the bottom, this is an acceptable layout change for responsive HTML, and the image itself is rendered correctly.

There is no genuine body content duplicated on the page. The appearance of the document title and major headings in both the sidebar navigation (Contents) and the main body is an intentional and expected accessible design feature for navigation landmarks.
